let me introduce Professor general or

I will highlight some of the recent

that's used for skin inspired devices

high performance with skin like
Properties Incorporated and they now
enable us to start building

some of the recent development in those

so today's electronics are mostly made
with silicon as the core material

result the electronic devices that we
use today are also rigid and Brito if we
look at the devices used for

either do not provide sufficient
information or they're very difficult to
to to interface with the human body to

even for implantable devices still very

and this severely limits the

applications
so what we envision is a future where
electronics are comfortable invisible in
perceptible
biocompatible and are able to collect
health related information autonomously
so to realize that goal we have been
pursuing the direction of we call
Electronic skiing or skiing inspired
Electronics where this large sheet
contains sensors and Signal processing
circuits and this large sensing sheet
takes the form factor of human skin so
that it can interface with the human

cause any damage to the tissue or the
surface it's in contact with
you may say that well for silicon it can
already integrate in high density and
very small feature size sensing
functionalities signal conditioning
circuits and the signal processing
circuits and wireless communication
circuits all in a small form factor and
why do we still need skin inspired
electronics that in terms of electronic

compete with that of single Crystal line
silicon but the um Advantage for such a
silicon device is its capability of high
level of integration uh so that means it
can collect information in a high
resolution in a very
um concentrated region meaning small
region
but what we are interested in is human
health and human health is regulated by
the communication between various
electrical signal and chemical
biochemical signal throughout the body
and these signals are generated at the
cellular level which is on the order of
a few microns to tens of Micron and
these feature sizes are much larger than
what the state of art silicon technology
is able to realize but on the other hand
the information is communicated through
the entire organ or through the entire
body so the area coverage can be a
centimeter Square to even meters square
so what we are really interested in is
to be able to measure information at
cellular level resolution but over a
very long large area and these are the
potential areas where we think skin
inspired Electronics can play a unique
role
so what we need is to begin with
materials that are as soft as jelly but
with electronic performance
in terms of conductive material as good
as metal or semiconductive material as
good as polysilical or amorphous silicon
single crystalline silica might be
difficult to realize but at least
amorphous silica and a polysilicon and
they will allow us to be able to build a
range of circuits
so my group has been working towards
that goal over the past more than a
decade we have two areas of focus one
area is focusing on materials
development because they are the
foundation for sensors and integrated
circuits and our latest circuit is the
one shown here and on the other hand we
also need a sensing functionality so we
started out with developing sensors that
can mimic sense of touch but now
expanding to chemical sensors
electrophysiology sensors and with the
ultimate goal of electronic skin which
combines the sensors as well as the
integrated circuits that we develop in
the group as the long-term goal for the
group but along the way there are many
devices and many applications that the
company components that we develop can
potentially enable
the key is the electronic material and
for any integrated circuits the
semiconducting material is the basic
most basic element that we need to build
transistors which are used to build
integrated circuits
and for the semiconducting material or
charge carrier Mobility is the parameter
that's used to to characterize the
ability for the transistor to switch at
a certain speed and to
to to operate to perform logic function
so the in terms of materials available
on top listed are the various inorganic
materials and on the bottom are various
organic materials in the polymers of
different format and through the um
fundamental Studies by the community of
organic Electronics the pink region
shaded region represents the charge
carrier Mobility that's realizable with
Organic electronic materials so you can
see in terms of electronic performance
organic materials already are
outperforming that of above a silicon
and entering into the regime of
polysilicum but the key materials design
challenge that we face is that now we
want to incorporate skin like properties
into the electronic materials but the
traditional design concept for polymer
semiconductors or polymer conductors is
to design rigid conjugated structures so
that it can facilitate
and so that it can facilitate the
intraction charge transport and then the
rigidity of the molecule also
facilitates pipei stacking so that there
can be good interchange charge transport
but this kind of molecular design result
in brittle polymerton films that forms
cracks at less than 25 strain on the
other hand for rubbery materials and
elastic materials the general molecular
design is to have flexible polymer
chains but this introduces a lot of
conformation disorder and slows down the
charge transport so these two
different requirements in molecular
design
to get to both properties same to
conflict with each other so our
challenge is to figure out how we can
come up with the molecular design that
address this seemingly conflicting
requirements
so the molecular design studies we have
been
carrying out over the years have been
trying to understand how we can balance
the charge transport that requires rigid
molecules crystalline
crystalline
morphology with trying to get amorphous
domains that will allow the mechanical
property but not having so much
amorphous domains that the electronic
property is being compromised so we look
at
systematically the polymer backbone
designed to introduce disorder through
various approaches such as introducing
the conjugation Breakers to lower the
rigidity or using fully conjugated rigid
species but then making them more
randomized so that then even though they
are fully conjugated by having random
co-monimer units reduces the overall
order also we can tune the side chance
to introduce disorders so various these
approaches have led to a number of
promising designs that provided
the
minimum decrease in the charge transport
level compared to
the fully rigid structure and the
crystalline structure there's less than
20 percent decrease in the charge
transport capability while at the same
time
introducing the stretchability
on the other hand we were also
interested in looking for more General
approach in polymer science a common
approach to
achieve two functions is to have a blend
in this case we need a semiconducting
property and the stretchability so it's
natural to think about having a blend
that has semiconductor and a stretchable
polymer but the problem with the
electronic polymer design is that this
kind of design for electronic conduction
will significantly degrade the
conduction because the insulating
material will take a fraction of the
conductive components and furthermore
the insulating material can get in
between the conductive components and
further reduce the charge transport so
it was very surprising when we first
made this blend uh we noticed that
compared to Pure 100 semiconducting
polymer uh even if with the addition of
70 weight percent of insulating polymer
in the blend we were able to see a
doubling in the charge carrier Mobility
which is completely unexpected and then
if we further control the coding
conditions to control the fluid flow
using this comb like structure that we
have previously been studying to control
the um uh the flow pattern we were able
to further boost the charge carrier
mobility of this kind of material so
that the mobility now reaches
enters into the regime of that of
polysilic
and upon further investigation we
discovered that in fact the uh with the
proper choice of the insulating polymer
here we want to have them to match the
surface energy of the semiconducting
polymer so that there is no dramatic
phase separation that will lead to
isolated semiconducting regions and then
on the other hand we noticed that
compared to the neat polymer which the
AFM shows a featureless structure but
with the addition of the insulating
polymer it results in this Nano confined
nanofiber structure and these structures
were already formed in solution because
in the solution you leave this we can
observe the aggregation
absorption Peak increase in its
intensity as we increase the amount of
insulating polymer we add into solution
so basically using additional Advanced
x-ray characterization we determined
that in this Nano confined structure
actually the polymer is forced to
into more planar conformation and to
reduce the confirmation or disorder and
this is a key to result in much improved
charge transport and of course the
details of film formation is also
important because this Nano confined
structure needed to be interconnected
and they have to be in good contact with
each other when the film is being formed
but with proper tuning we are able to
obtain such a structure and with the
Boost in the charge transport instead
instead of decreasing in the charge
transport and at the same time we are
obtained the stretchability
so with these various molecular design
approach and especially the Nano
confinement we're able to significantly
boost the charge transport of organic
semiconductors to beyond that of the
amorphous silicon level and entering
into polysilican regime
and we are very excited about this
discovery of Nano confinement effect
because it's a key for us to add other
skin-like properties without degrading
the electronic property and even
enhancing it for example adding the um
biodegradability and the elasticity can
be achieved by using a matrix that's

and for self-healable and a stretchable

the suitable polymer semiconductor and
its Matrix so that it can also form the

case
um the um having a dynamic polymer

originally the semiconducting polymer

and going from semiconducting material
to conductive material this Nano
confinement effect again allowed us to

such as high conductivity this is a

and then addition of the stretchability

allows the suppression of the

photo Crosslink
can be a photo cross-linkable groups

conductivity that at a level of indium

confinement Nano confinement effect and
